explanation: <Child> has an irregular plural: <children>. The verb changes from <is> to <are>.
<Man> has an irregular plural: <men>. The verb changes from <is> to <are>.
<Woman> has an irregular plural: <women>. The noun <doctor> also becomes <doctors>.
Nouns ending in <-x> add <-es> to form the plural: <boxes>.
Nouns ending in <-s> add <-es> to form the plural: <buses>.
Remove the <-s> from <dogs> and change <are> to <is>.
Nouns ending in <-ies> change back to <-y> in singular: <baby>.
Nouns ending in consonant + <y> change <y> to <ies>: <cities>.
Nouns ending in <-fe> change to <-ves>: <knives>.
Nouns ending in <-f> change to <-ves>: <leaves>.
<Mice> is the irregular plural of <mouse>.
<Tooth> has an irregular plural: <teeth>. The verb <hurt> stays the same.
<Foot> has an irregular plural: <feet>.

source: The fish is in the water.
correct: The fish are in the water.
explanation: <Fish> has the same form in singular and plural. Only the verb changes.
<Sheep> has the same form in singular and plural. Only the verb changes.
<People> is the irregular plural of <person>.
Some nouns ending in <-o> add <-es>: <tomatoes>.

source: The potato is on the table.
correct: The potatoes are on the table.
explanation: Some nouns ending in <-o> add <-es>: <potatoes>.
Some nouns ending in <-o> just add <-s>: <photos>.
Nouns ending in <-ch> add <-es>: <watches>.
Nouns ending in <-sh> add <-es>: <dishes>.

source: The geese are in the park.
correct: The goose is in the park.
explanation: <Geese> is the irregular plural of <goose>.
Nouns ending in <-ss> add <-es>: <classes>.
Nouns ending in consonant + <y> change <y> to <ies>: <stories>.
<Wolves> changes back to <wolf> in singular. Nouns ending in <-ves> become <-f>.
